topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts aimed at reforming and regulating student loan practices and protections for borrowers.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an exclusion from gross income for the payment of an employer...
Defines 'qualified education refinance loan' as any indebtedness used solely to refinance a qualified education loan.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to limit the unpaid amount of Direct Stafford loans...
Caps the annual interest rate for Direct Stafford loans at 6.8%.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private education loans to describe clearly...
Directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to publish a model form for describing a cosigner's obligations.
Document 4: Requires institutions of higher education to provide to each student with education loans for attending the institution an estimate of student loan borrowing costs...
The estimates must be given to students before each academic year succeeding the one for which a loan was first received.
Document 5: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to repeal the Secretary of Education's authority...
Authorizes the Secretary to offer borrowers FFEL Debt Conversion Loans, until October 2012...
Document 6: Declares that, if a veteran is forced to discontinue a course as a result of an educational institution's permanent closure...
The VA may also continue to pay a monthly housing stipend following a permanent school closure.
Document 7: Establishes new programs for teacher student loan forgiveness under the guaranteed loan program...
Makes eligible for such student loan forgiveness any new borrower on or after October 1, 1998...

topic.name: Voting and Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses legislative measures aimed at protecting voting rights and ensuring fair labor practices in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unfair labor practice for an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization that has not been selected by a majority of the employees in a unit appropriate for such purposes in a secret ballot election.
Requires the NLRB to conduct a secret ballot election to determine whether a labor organization certified or recognized by an employer is no longer the representative of a unit.
Document 2: Prohibits state or local election officials from preventing an individual from registering or voting in any election for federal office.
Defines 'voter caging document' and outlines conditions under which an individual's registration status can be challenged.
Document 3: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act to require the presidential designee to have experience in election administration.
Establishes the Overseas Voting Advisory Board to assist U.S. citizens residing outside the United States in voting.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation.
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the NLRB.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act to permit candidates to designate individuals for their campaign committees who will manage funds in the event of the candidate's death.
Prescribes procedures for filing and revoking such designations.
Document 6: Prohibits a state from imposing an income tax on the compensation of a nonresident individual for any period in which such individual is not physically present in or working in such state.
Addresses the tax implications of telecommuting.
Document 7: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act to prohibit a state from certifying election results until all valid ballots of absent servicemember voters are counted.
Allows a state to certify results on a specific timeline after the election.

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Transportation Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting renewable energy, reducing greenhouse gas emissions, and advancing clean transportation technologies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate regulations that exempt from lifecycle greenhouse gas (GHG) emission requirements
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a program to provide loan guarantees for the construction of facilities for the manufacture of advanced vehicle batteries
provide grants to state governments, local governments, metropolitan transportation authorities
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a methodology for use in determining the lifecycle greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions of transportation fuel
establish a transportation fuel certification and marketing process concerning specified fuels
Document 4: Amends the Federal Power Act to direct the President to designate an area as a National Renewable Energy Zone
requires the President to identify...additional renewable energy trunkline facilities
Document 5: Amends the Clean Air Act to establish national pollutant tonnage limitations for sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxides, mercury, and carbon dioxide
directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a nitrogen oxide allowance trading program
Document 6: Instructs the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to publish in the Federal Register an oil savings target and action plan
directs the Secretary of Transportation to develop a fuel efficiency consumer education program for motor vehicle tires
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Energy to conduct a national media campaign to decrease oil consumption in the United States
requires a specified graduated percentage of passenger cars manufactured by light-duty motor vehicles manufacturers to be fuel choice enabling motor vehicles

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at modifying and improving the Medicare and Medicaid programs, focusing on provider regulations, payment adjustments, and patient care enhancements.

Document 1: establish limits on the removal of Medicare Advantage (MA) providers
requires a fair notice and appeal process
Document 2: modifies payment rules and other provisions related to the Medicare and Medicaid programs
expands criminal penalties and civil monetary penalties for certain acts involving federal health care programs
Document 3: repeal the prohibition against payment of federal funds to a state for medical assistance expenditures
requires application of certain reporting requirements and sanctions
Document 4: extend permanently the authorization for certain Medicare Advantage (MA) Dual Special Needs Plans
develop a clearly defined role for state Medicaid agencies
Document 5: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to transfer funds for high risk health insurance pool program
eliminates the eligibility requirement for the temporary high risk pool
Document 6: allow uninsured individual taxpayers a refundable tax credit for health insurance costs
expand Medicaid health opportunity accounts
Document 7: increases Medicare payments to qualifying Medicare providers by 10% for cancer screening tests
waive cost-sharing for colorectal cancer screening tests

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the criminal justice system through increased penalties, improved investigative tools, and support for law enforcement agencies.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to develop a plan to assist states in performing DNA analyses of samples collected from convicted child sex offenders.
providing for entry of those analyses into the combined DNA Indexing System (CODIS).
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to impose enhanced criminal penalties for serious violent felonies committed during and in relation to any drug trafficking crime.
establish limitation periods for prosecuting certain noncapital crimes of violence and terrorism.
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to designate a Deputy Chief in the Criminal Section of the Civil Rights Division of the Department of Justice (DOJ).
Authorizes the Attorney General to award grants to state or local law enforcement agencies for the investigation and prosecution of such cases.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to subject all illegal alien entrants to fines.
Requires certain airport security screeners to undergo immigration status checks.
Document 5: Authorizes and directs the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security to expand resources, make grants, and take other actions to protect U.S. citizens and property.
Directs the Attorney General to dedicate and expand resources for the Project Gunrunner initiative of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF).
Document 6: Amends the federal criminal code to expand mail and wire fraud prohibitions to include fraudulent use of the mails or wire to obtain any thing of value.
Directs the U.S. Sentencing Commission to review and amend its guidelines and policy statements to reflect congressional intent to increase penalties for public corruption.
Document 7: Amends the federal criminal code to increase or impose new criminal penalties for the use of firearms to commit a crime of violence or a drug trafficking crime.
Imposes mandatory restitution for victims of violent crime.

topic.name: Iran Sanctions Legislation
topic.description: A collection of U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on Iran for its nuclear program, support of terrorism, and human rights abuses.

Document 1: Imposes sanctions with respect to facilitating the extraction or milling of uranium ore in Iran.
Subjects a parent entity to penalties for violations of certain sanctions regarding Iran.
Document 2: Prohibits a federal banking agency from suggesting or ordering a depository institution to terminate customer accounts based on reputation risk related to national security.
Deems criteria met if a customer is believed to be involved in terrorist financing.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to report on efforts to ensure that SWIFT has terminated services to the Central Bank of Iran.
Authorizes the President to impose specified sanctions on entities that maintain relationships with Iranian financial institutions.
Document 4: Prescribes requirements for sanctions with respect to Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps and related entities.
Requires the Department of the Treasury to publish an IRGC watch list.
Document 5: Prohibits the President from paying final judgments to Iran until certain conditions are met.
Imposes sanctions against any Iranian person involved in the kidnapping or unjust detention of a U.S. citizen.
Document 6: Prohibits the U.S. government from providing monetary instruments to Iran.
Requires specific licensing for transactions related to claims against Iran.
Document 7: Declares the sense of Congress about sanctions for Iran's illicit nuclear activities.
Requires foreign companies to be blocked from entering into joint ventures with Iranian entities.

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety, security, and operational efficiency within the aviation sector.

Document 1: Revises Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) requirements for the hiring of air traffic controllers.

Document 2: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (TSA) to establish in the TSA an aviation security advisory committee.

Document 3: Directs the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to develop and implement a preventive maintenance validation process for security-related technology deployed to airports.
The DHS Inspector General shall assess implementation of the requirements of this Act.
Document 4: Directs the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to establish annual Next Generation Air Transportation System (NextGen) performance goals.

Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend increased excise taxes on aviation fuels.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. agricultural policies and conservation efforts aimed at enhancing food security, ecosystem restoration, and wildlife protection.

Document 1: Defines specified terms related to commodity programs and conservation payments.

Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reauthorize the Chesapeake Bay Program.

Document 3: Addresses wildlife trafficking and the illegal trade in wildlife products.

Document 5: Requires the President to develop a Global Food Security Strategy to promote global food security and nutrition.
Specifies goals for agricultural growth and reducing global poverty and hunger.
Document 6: Prohibits direct payments to individuals with high adjusted gross income and links payments to environmental stewardship.

Document 7: Establishes the Federal Land Assistance, Management, and Enhancement Fund for emergency wildland fire suppression activities.

Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase from $50,000 to $320,000 the amount of employer-provided group term life insurance

topic.name: Water and Environmental Regulation

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments for Exempt Organizations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax benefits and incentives for specific groups, including artists, veterans, senior volunteers, and first-time homebuyers.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain support organizations for tax-exempt teaching hospitals to acquire debt secured by income-producing real property used to provide financial support for such hospitals.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers who create literary, musical, artistic, or scholarly compositions or similar property a fair market value tax deduction for contributions of such properties.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individuals who have attained age 60 or are disabled an exemption from income and employment taxes for real property tax abatements.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income payments under the federal veterinary medicine loan repayment program.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2018 the tax credit for differential wage payments made by a small business employer.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers who are part-time students to claim the tax credit for dependent care expenses.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an individual taxpayer who qualifies as a first-time homebuyer a one-time refundable credit for up to $20,000.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Tribes
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at providing federal recognition, benefits, and services to various Native American tribes and their members.

Document 4: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to transfer land the federal government holds in trust for a federally recognized Indian tribe...

Document 6: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
